---
title: "A Thesis (Skripsi) with Quarto"
subtitle: "One project → a thesis PDF in your campus format (FEB UNDIP)"
lang: en
---

> 🌐 **Versi Bahasa Indonesia:** [baca dalam Bahasa Indonesia](../skripsi.qmd)

Quarto isn't just for plain documents — it can produce a **full thesis** with a **print
layout exactly like a real skripsi**: cover page, approval page, Chapters I–V, automatic
numbering, lists of contents/tables/figures, and a bibliography. You write the content and
analysis; Quarto handles the formatting.

## The result: a true thesis layout {#sec-layout}

The example below is rendered from the thesis template (Central Java poverty case study) —
**its print layout matches a real skripsi**:

::: {layout-ncol=2}
![Cover page — title, "SKRIPSI", FEB Universitas Diponegoro, name & student ID](../img/skripsi-cover.png){.border}

![Numbered chapter + automatic **Table 5.1** & coloured cross-references](../img/skripsi-bab.png){.border}
:::

Figures, maps, and statistical tests appear in thesis layout too — all **computed from the
data**, never pasted by hand:

![Figure 5.1 (map with OSM basemap) + classical-assumption tests, in thesis layout](../img/skripsi-peta.png){.border width="62%" fig-align="center"}

## What Quarto handles automatically

- **Front matter**: cover, approval page, abstract, preface.
- **Numbering**: "Chapter I–V", **Table 5.1 / Figure 5.1**, equations — with clickable
  **cross-references**.
- **Automatic lists**: table of contents, list of tables, list of figures.
- **Citations & bibliography**: APA (or other) styles, linked to **Zotero/`.bib`**.
- **Analysis results**: descriptive tables, charts, maps, assumption tests, regression —
  straight from code + data (see the **[Data Analysis](analisis.qmd)** page).

## Thesis project structure

```
thesis/
├── _quarto.yml          # "book" project: chapter order, PDF/Word format
├── index.qmd            # title page / abstract
├── bab/
│   ├── 01-pendahuluan.qmd      # Chapter I (Introduction)
│   ├── 02-tinjauan-pustaka.qmd # Chapter II (Literature)
│   ├── 03-metode.qmd           # Chapter III (Methods)
│   ├── 04-hasil.qmd            # Chapter IV/V (Results & Discussion)
│   └── 05-penutup.qmd
├── data/                # your data (.csv, .geojson)
├── tex/preamble.tex     # LaTeX style (margins, Times font, chapter numbering)
└── referensi.bib        # references (export from Zotero)
```

## How to use it

1. **Grab a ready template** (R or Python) — see *Downloads* below.
2. **Write each chapter** in `bab/*.qmd` (plain Markdown + code blocks for analysis).
3. **Swap in your data** under `data/`.
4. **Render** → `thesis PDF`. (For Word: `--to docx`.)
5. **Data/variables change?** Re-render — tables, charts, maps, numbers, and the
   bibliography **update automatically**.

::: {.callout-tip}
## Match your campus format
Margins, font (e.g. **Times New Roman 12pt**), 1.5 line spacing, and numbering live in
`tex/preamble.tex` and `_quarto.yml` — set them once, applied across the whole thesis.
:::

## Downloads & templates

- 📄 **[Download the example thesis (PDF)](../skripsi/contoh-skripsi.pdf)** — 35 pages,
  rendered from the template.
- 📦 **Thesis templates (R & Python)** + defense slides — in the book's repo:
  <https://github.com/firmanhadi21/thesis-with-quarto>
- 📘 **Full book "Quarto untuk Skripsi"** (chapter-by-chapter guide; Indonesian):
  <https://firmanhadi21.github.io/thesis-with-quarto/>
